seoTitle: How to Run QuickBooks Payroll Reports
metaDescription: Learn how to run and customize payroll reports in QuickBooks so you can review wages, taxes, and deductions without digging through pay stubs.
intro: Payroll data is easier to review as a report than as a stack of individual pay stubs. This guide walks through generating and customizing payroll reports in QuickBooks so you can quickly extract the numbers you need.

steps.heading: Open the payroll reports section
steps.body: Go to Reports and find the payroll category, which lists the different payroll report types available.

steps.heading: Choose the report you need
steps.body: Select a report based on what you're reviewing, such as wage summaries, tax liabilities, or deductions.

steps.heading: Set the date range and employees
steps.body: Filter the report to the pay period or employees relevant to your review.

steps.heading: Run and export the report
steps.body: Generate the report on screen, then export or print it if you need to share or archive it.

faq.question: What payroll report types are available?
faq.answer: Common options include payroll summary, payroll details, tax liability, and deduction reports, among others.

faq.question: Can I run a payroll report for a single employee?
faq.answer: Yes, most payroll reports let you filter down to one or a select group of employees.

faq.question: Do payroll reports show tax withholdings?
faq.answer: Yes, several payroll report types break out tax withholdings alongside gross and net pay.

faq.question: Can I export payroll reports to Excel?
faq.answer: Yes, payroll reports typically support exporting to Excel or PDF just like other QuickBooks reports.
